title: 44 Digital Commons Projects Selected for NGI Zero Grants
raw_excerpt: We are happy to announce that 44 Free and Open Source projects have been selected to become beneficiaries from the NGI Zero Commons Fund. The selection covers the entire technology stack from trustworthy open hardware to services & applications providing user autonomy.

candidate_recipients: 44 FOSS projects (NGI Zero Commons Fund cohort)

title: NLnet Foundation Selected for Three Horizon Europe Grants
raw_excerpt: NLnet has been selected for one Cascade Funding and two Pilot programmes. The first is the Open Internet Stack call, a new 10M€ initiative enabling NLnet and consortium partners to continue NGI Zero. The two Pilots focus on local-first technologies and software.

title: NLnet Receives Large Donation from Proton Foundation
raw_excerpt: Each year in December Proton Foundation runs the Lifetime account fundraiser for online freedom. As one of ten beneficiaries, NLnet will receive 100,000 euros and direct it toward its mission of a free, open, resilient and human internet.

title: Schmidt Sciences awards over $3 million to study AI's impact on jobs
raw_excerpt: Researcher-led field experiments will explore how AI can support rather than supplant humans in a variety of workplaces worldwide. As part of its AI at Work program, Schmidt Sciences has awarded over $3M (Jan 27, 2026).

candidate_recipients: Multiple researchers (AI at Work program cohort; recipient list not parsed from listing)

title: AISI Challenge Fund (priority areas, application pack)
raw_excerpt: AISI grants page now lists the AISI Challenge Fund alongside the Alignment Project, with linked PDFs for priority areas, application pack, and clarification questions. Distinct from the (currently closed) Alignment Project.

title: Sovereign Tech Fellowship 2026: Applications are open
raw_excerpt: Sovereign Tech Agency has opened applications for the 2026 Sovereign Tech Fellowship, continuing direct support to maintainers of critical open-source infrastructure.

title: Call for Tenders: Sovereign Tech Resilience Program
raw_excerpt: Sovereign Tech Agency issued a Call for Tenders for the Sovereign Tech Resilience Program, supporting hardening of foundational open-source infrastructure.

title: NSF invests $11M to expand AI professional development for K-12 teachers nationwide
raw_excerpt: NSF announced an $11M investment to expand AI professional development for K-12 teachers nationwide. AI-education-adjacent; relevance to the open-source AI stack is borderline and needs human judgment.

title: Build in the Open: Funding the Future of Trustworthy Tech
raw_excerpt: Mozilla Foundation post signals a new era of technical grantmaking. Not a specific grant award; surfaces direction for upcoming Mozilla programs. Worth a human read to decide if it warrants a funders.yaml update.

title: MATS Autumn 2026 Fellowship applications now open
raw_excerpt: ML Alignment & Theory Scholars (MATS) Autumn 2026 fellowship cohort applications now open. MATS is a recurring AI-safety research fellowship; funder not currently profiled in funders.yaml.

title: NSF announces $1.5B NSF X-Labs initiative to pursue generational breakthrough science efforts
raw_excerpt: NSF announced $1.5 billion over the next decade for the NSF X-Labs initiative to tackle pressing scientific challenges. AI/compute relevance not confirmed from listing alone; needs human read to determine whether any X-Labs themes touch open-source AI infrastructure, compute, evaluation, or safety.

title: Anthropic and the Gates Foundation $200M partnership
raw_excerpt: Anthropic and the Gates Foundation announce a $200M partnership. Likely focused on AI for global health applications. Edge case for the open-source AI stack: Anthropic is a closed-weights lab, but the funding flow itself (a major foundation putting $200M into AI deployment) is signal for funder mapping. Human should decide whether this belongs in funders/grants or is out of scope.
